overview: The Village of Weston Parks Committee approved installation of new kayak launch signs along the Eau Claire River, deferred discussion on allowing dogs at the aquatic center until next month, and received updates on Kennedy Park fundraising and trail maintenance. The meeting addressed public concerns about fundraising transparency and meeting scheduling.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Yellow Banks disc golf course update
Dog Day at the aquatic center
Graffiti on the Eau Claire River Trail
Topics for future meetings and staff remarks

discussions.body: Minutes were approved with a motion and second. Motion carried with all in favor, no opposition.

Sean Osterbrink presented a timeline of the Kennedy Park project from its inception through the May 9th ribbon cutting. He explained the village plans to contribute approximately $7 million of the $14.3 million total project for infrastructure items like bathrooms, shelter, and wellhouse. The Friends of Kennedy Park nonprofit is fundraising separately with a 10-year timeline. They currently have approximately $84,000-85,000 at the community foundation toward a $117,000 goal for field lights, and plan a public check presentation at their June tournament. Committee members discussed that the project is designed as a long-range fundraiser with no fixed completion deadline.

discussions.item: Yellow Banks disc golf course update
discussions.body: Dan presented information on tree removal from the river and mat installation over gas pipelines. Photos showed wood mats placed to protect pipelines during equipment operation. The mats are now village property with extras available for other trail projects. TC Energy (formerly ANR Pipeline/ATC) conducted site inspections during the work.

discussions.item: Dog Day at the aquatic center
discussions.body: The committee deferred this item to the June meeting because Committee Chair Katrina was absent due to an unforeseen issue. One audience member (Andy, 6504 [street name inaudible]) had attended specifically to oppose allowing dogs at the public pool, stating 'There should not be animals allowed into a public pool. Period.'

discussions.item: Graffiti on the Eau Claire River Trail
discussions.body: Staff explained challenges removing graffiti from concrete and memorial benches due to limited access for pressure washing equipment. The best removal tool is a sandblaster through a pressure washer, but the trailer cannot reach the trail. Committee discussed anti-vandalism coatings and seal coat options. Committee member suggested volunteer work days, noting community members love the trail. No formal action taken; staff will investigate cleaning options.

Dan Wierzba (2625 Northwestern Avenue) presented version 7 of proposed signage for Eau Claire River kayak launches. Three signs will be in Village of Weston (Highway J, Ross Avenue, Yellow Banks) and two or three in City of Schofield. Signs include QR codes for river gauge and Marathon County Parks Foundation donation link. Highway J launch may be closed all season due to bridge replacement. Motion to approve sign installation passed unanimously.

Staff requested committee interest in food allergy awareness signage for playgrounds, noting one in 13 people have food allergies. Committee agreed to bring this back for future discussion. The Mock Miller master plan process will begin with a kickoff meeting with Rettler, and June 11th is the last open house for the bike and pedestrian plan.

publicComment: Two members of the public commented. Andy (6504 [street inaudible]) spoke in person opposing dogs at the aquatic center, stating dogs belong in rivers and lakes, not public pools. Jim Pensan (5002 [street] Weston), appearing online under the name 'Vern M', criticized the 4 p.m. meeting time as discouraging public participation, questioned the Kennedy Park funding report accuracy (noting the $8,700 figure seemed too coincidental), asked where the $10,000 grant money and Glow event funds were, and accused the Friends of Kennedy Park of being 'convoluted and misleading' with their finances.
actionItems: Kayak launch sign installation approved - will go to village board
Dog Day at aquatic center discussion deferred to June meeting
Staff to investigate graffiti removal and cleaning options for Eau Claire River Trail
Food allergy awareness playground signage to be brought back for future discussion
Mock Miller master plan kickoff meeting with Rettler scheduled for next Tuesday
title: Parks Committee
